GET TO KNOW BAYLEE KLINGLER

Goals in life: To win the Women's College World Series, play in the Olympics, and travel the world

Three words that her friends would use to describe her: hard working, loyal, and passionate

What it means to represent the United States: It means the world to me to represent the U.S. in softball. We live in the best country and to be able to wear those three letters on my chest is an honor.

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Emily and Jeffrey
  • She has one sister, Nettie-Clare, who plans to dive for the University of Wyoming
  • Her interests and hobbies include reading, photography, crocheting, scrapbooking, journaling, and camping
  • Her father, Jeffrey, was an All-American defensive lineman for University of Wyoming and was drafted in the sixth round by the Los Angeles Rams in 1987
  • Her mother, Emily, is the co-author Get Set for School by Learning Without Tears
  • Abi began diving at 14 years. She had started gymnastics when she was three and was a Level 8 gymnast
  • For the last 10 years of her career, Abi had to drive 4-6 hours to train on a platform but now has full access to a tower after following her coaches to New Jersey
  • Says having tower access has definitely shifted the way she trains, both mentally and physically, over the last 6 months
  • She would like to become a Nurse Practitioner
  • Between dives, Abi likes to read and listen to music
  • Her favorite artist to listen to is Imagine Dragons, and she also likes classic rock and oldies
  • Her favorite dive is anything with a reverse
  • If she could do anything outside of diving, she would love to be an archeologist because she grew up watching Indiana Jones and wanted to be just like him

  • She describes herself as passionate, creative and determined
  • Her favorite TV shows are Grey's Anatomy and Friends
  • Her favorite movie is Footloose
  • If Abi could meet anyone, it would be Lily James

bio.fun_fact: Enjoys playing piano, singing, building Lego sets, strategic board games and science fiction films
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Specialty: Para Nordic Skiing (Biathlon and Cross-Country) — Sitting Classification (LW11.5)
  • One of only 20 high school graduates to be awarded Montana State’s Presidential Scholarship, the university’s most prestigious scholarship
  • Began skiing at age 17
  • Parents, Linda and Paul and he has five siblings: Fred, Merrily, Jessica, David and Chen
  • Enjoys playing piano, singing, building Lego sets, strategic board games and science fiction films
Biographical Information

Michael Kneeland is known in American Para Nordic skiing for the speed with which he has ascended to the international level. Kneeland came to competitive skiing relatively late by elite standards, picking up the sport at age 17. Bozeman's outdoor culture and proximity to mountain terrain gave him consistent access to training conditions, and his natural athleticism and analytical mindset accelerated his progress through the para Nordic development pipeline.

Born with a condition that led to bilateral rotationplasty, a surgical procedure in which both legs were rotated 180 degrees, repositioning the ankle joint to function as a knee. He competes in the LW11.5 sitting classification and uses a sit-ski to compete in cross-country skiing and biathlon events.

In January 2024, while still a high school junior, he traveled to Italy and competed in his first two world cup events. In February 2025, he competed at the IBU Para Biathlon World Championships in Pokljuka, Slovenia, becoming the youngest of seven Americans racing at the event and the youngest competitor in his classification. Throughout the 2024-25 season, Kneeland continued to improve, competing at world cup events across Europe and gaining experience at venues he would later see at the Paralympics.

Kneeland made his world championships debut at the 2025 IBU Para Biathlon World Championships in Pokljuka, Slovenia, where he placed 19th in the 7.5-kilometer biathlon pursuit and 21st in the sprint biathlon pursuit in the sitting men's classification. 

In February 2026, Kneeland received the news that would validate his years of training and sacrifice: he was awarded bipartite spots (invitation-based qualification) by both the International Ski Federation and International Biathlon Union to compete at the Paralympic Winter Games Milano Cortina 2026.

Off the snow, Kneeland is an exceptional student. He attended Heritage Christian School in Bozeman, where he distinguished himself not only as an emerging Para Nordic talent but also as robotics team captain, student government member, award-winning vocalist, and theater performer. Named one of only 20 recipients of Montana State's most prestigious Presidential Scholarship from a pool of 1,434 applicants, Kneeland earned the honor based on scholastic achievement, demonstrated leadership, and exemplary public service. His academic focus reflects a curiosity about how things work that parallels his approach to athletic performance: precise, technical, and analytical.

Beyond sport and academics, Kneeland channels his creativity into music and his love of collecting Lego sets. He describes strategic board games as some of his most enjoyable ways to unwind, and his taste in film leans toward science fiction, reflecting a broader fascination with technology and imagined futures that extends naturally into his engineering studies.

World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – 19th (7.5km Sprint - Sitting - Men), 21th (Sprint Pursuit - Sitting - Men)
  • Years of participation: 7.5km Sprint - Sitting - Men 2025; Sprint Pursuit - Sitting - Men 2025
  • Top finish: 19th – 2025 (7.5km Sprint - Sitting - Men)
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Milan 2026, (Sprint Pursuit - Sitting - Men), 11th (4 x 2.5km Relay Open - Open), 19th (7.5km Sprint - Sitting - Men), 25th (10km - Sitting - Men), 32th (1km Sprint - Sitting - Men)

US Figure Skating

Quick Facts

  • Position: Left Wing

  • Son of Miro and Michaela Knies

  • Has an older brother, Phil who is also a hockey player

  • Drafted 57th overall by the Toronto Maple Leafs in the 2021 NHL Entry Draft

  • Played collegiate hockey at the University of Minnesota, earning All-Big Ten honors

  • Made his NHL debut with the Toronto Maple Leafs in April 2023

  • Fluent in Slovak

Biographical Information 

Matthew Knies is an American professional ice hockey forward. Born in Phoenix, Arizona, he developed a passion for hockey at a young age, inspired by his family's love for the sport. Knies played collegiate hockey at the University of Minnesota, where he earned All-Big Ten honors and showcased his scoring ability and physical style of play.

Drafted 57th overall by the Toronto Maple Leafs in the 2021 National Hockey League (NHL) Entry Draft, Knies made his NHL debut in April 2023. In the 2024-25 season, he distinguished himself as one of only three players to record 25 goals and 150 hits, joining the ranks of Tom Wilson and Brady Tkachuk.

    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, 5th (Team - Men)

  • Hobbies include baking, running and hanging out with friends.

  • World Junior Championships, Trakai, 2017 (Pair - Women)

    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 5th (Four - Women)

bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Position: Forward
  • Daughter of James and Cynthia Knight
  • She has three younger brothers, James Jr., Remington and William
  • Her cousin, Chip Knight, was a three-time Olympian for Team USA as an an alpine skier
  • Began skating at age two and started playing ice hockey at five
  • Enjoys playing pickleball and golf
  • Wears No. 21 as a tribute to Cammi Granato
Biographical Information

Hilary Knight was introduced to hockey at age five (when her family moved to Illinois) and is one of the most decorated athletes in the sport. She played college hockey at the University of Wisconsin, winning two national titles and becoming the program’s all-time leader in points and goals scored, coached by former 1980 "Miracle on Ice" hero Mark Johnson.

Knight made her Olympic debut at the Olympic Winter Games Vancouver 2010 as the youngest member of the silver medal-winning team. Knight is a record 10-time world champion and owns all three scoring milestone records (goals, assists and points) in IIHF Women's Hockey World Championships history. Knight is a four-time Olympic medalist, winning gold with Team USA at the Olympic Winter Games PyeongChang 2018.

Off the ice, Knight is focused on her golf shot and enjoys pickleball. She is a leading advocate for gender equity in sports and played a key role in creating the Professional Women’s Hockey League (PWHL).

Olympic Experience
  • 5-time Olympian; 5-time Olympic medalist (2 gold, 3 silver)
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, gold (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, silver (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Pyeongchang 2018, gold (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Sochi 2014, silver (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Vancouver 2010, silver (Team - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – gold (Team - Women)
  • Years of participation: Team - Women 2007, 2008, 2009, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2019, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025
  • Medals: 15 (10 gold, 5 silver)
    • Gold – 2025 (Team - Women); 2023 (Team - Women); 2019 (Team - Women); 2017 (Team - Women); 2016 (Team - Women); 2015 (Team - Women); 2013 (Team - Women); 2011 (Team - Women); 2009 (Team - Women); 2008 (Team - Women)
    • Silver – 2024 (Team - Women); 2022 (Team - Women); 2021 (Team - Women); 2012 (Team - Women); 2007 (Team - Women)

bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Eric and Bonnie Knight
  • Has a twin brother, O'Brien, and an older sister, Delanie
  • Enjoys hiking and watching any beautiful sunrise or sunset
  • Started diving as a sophomore in high school after being done with gymnastics the year before
  • Originally didn't think she'd start diving as she wanted to run track year-round
  • 2018 North Carolina high school state diving champion
  • Likes to listen to music (country or pop), color on her phone or watch TikToks in between dives
  • Favorite dive is 5152B
  • If she could meet anyone, she'd love to meet Carrie Underwood

Quick Facts

  • Sport: Track and field 

  • Specialty: 100-meter and 200-meter sprints

Biographical Information

Erriyon Knighton is a phenomenal talent in U.S. track and field, specializing in sprint events. Knighton has broken numerous age-group records and emerged as one of the most promising sprinters in the world. His rapid rise in the sport has drawn comparisons to legendary sprinters and positioned him as a potential future star of American athletics.

Knighton attended Hillsborough High School, where he initially focused on football. His transition to track and field came later, but his natural speed quickly became apparent. In his sophomore year, he began to focus more seriously on sprinting and his times improved rapidly.

Olympic History 

Knighton made his Olympic debut at the Tokyo 2020 Olympic Games. At just 17 years old, he competed in the men’s 200-meter. He became the youngest male track and field athlete to compete for the U.S. in the Olympics since Jim Ryun in 1964. In the finals of the U.S. Olympic Trials, he set a world record time of 19.84 seconds. He advanced to the final, where he finished in fourth place with a time of 19.93 seconds. 

Knighton made his second Olympic appearance at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games, competing in the men’s 200-meter. After winning his semi-final race, he placed 4th in the men’s 200-meter final, one spot behind American counterpart, Noah Lyles, with a time of 19.99 seconds.

World Championship Highlights

Knighton made his mark on the world stage at the 2022 World Championships in Eugene, Oregon. Competing in the 200-meter final, he delivered a standout performance and clinched the bronze medal with a time of 19.77 seconds. He then went on to win silver at the 2023 World Championships, with a time of 19.75 seconds.

Early Career

His breakthrough came in 2020 when he broke the 15-16 age group world record in the 200-meter, a mark previously held by Usain Bolt. This performance put him on the radar of the track and field world and set the stage for his ascent to the elite level of the sport.

In 2021, at the age of 17, Knighton set a new world under-18 best record for the 200-meter with a time of 20.11 seconds, beating Usain Bolt’s best time. He continued to improve his time in qualifying events during the U.S. Olympic Trials, bringing his record down to 19.84 seconds. This time secured his place on the Olympic team.

In 2022, he set a new 200-meter world junior record with a time of 19.49 seconds. He also achieved the title of youngest winner of a Diamond League race.

    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 4th (200m - Men)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 4th (200m - Men)

Mike Kohn Assistant Bobsled Coach

As an assistant bobsled coach with USA Bobsled & Skeleton, Mike Kohn is responsible for leading, managing and mentoring the National Team athletes, as well as effectively and proactively communicate with all USABS constituents, including athletes, USABS staff and partners. Kohn is also responsible for recruiting, as well as introducing and integrating new athletes into the USABS system. He is required to provide discipline, motivation, guidance and empathy to athletes at all levels from rookies up to and including Olympic medalists. Furthermore, he must provide continuous feedback and coaching to pilots and brakemen, ensuring each athlete exceedes his/her goals and expectations for the season. Kohn also must understand team selection criteria, the policies and procedures of the USABS Athlete Handbook, the organization's bylaws, professional code, and maintain a working relationship with the USABS and its constituents.

Mike Kohn is a 1st Lt. in the Virginia Army National Guard and a former member of the National Guard Outstanding Athlete Program. Kohn joined the National Guard in 1999 as an Infantry Soldier, and is one of several U.S. Olympic bobsled-soldier athletes that have benefited from the Outstanding Athlete Program. Kohn began the sport of bobsled during his senior year in High School when Coach Carlino wrote him a letter on June 6, 1990 welcoming him to the team. Kohn was the youngest competitor in the 1991 Olympic Trials, and went on to win the 2002 Olympic four-man bronze medal with driver and current USABS bobsled coach, Brian Shimer. Kohn was also named to the 2006 Olympic team as an alternate, and competed in the 2010 Olympics as a driver. He retired following the 2010 Vancouver Winter Olympic Games.

Mike was named among People Magazine's "Top 50 Most Eligible Bachelors" in 2002. In 2002, Mike visited Afghanistan as a representative of the National Guard. Mike's brother, Nicholas, is a Broadway performer. He is a son to a former Green Beret in the U.S. Army. He admires his parents for their passion for life and commitment to excellence in everything they do. He enjoys golf and riding his Harley-Davidson.  Mike graduated from George Mason University in1997 with a degree in Health, Fitness and Recreation Management. Kohn is also a Certified Conditioning Specialist with the National Strength Professionals Association.

  • 1999-2000 season                                                                                                                                    -Brakeman with driver Todd Hays during World Cup. Placed 15th in three of four World Cup races
  • 1998-1999 season                                                                                                                                     -Brakeman with driver Brian Shimer.                                                                                                      -5th at World Championships in Cortina, Italy ( 02/99)

    • Silver – 2007 (Team Competition - Mixed)

Quick Facts
  • I started karate at the age of 7 in Honolulu, Hawaii
  • I made the U.S. Junior National Team when I was 14 and have been representing the country ever since.
  • When I was younger, I just loved being around people in the dojo and loved traveling to international events with my teammates
  • As I got older, I started to set bigger goals for myself as an athlete and fell in love with the sport all over again
  • To pursue my Olympic dream, I moved to California to stay with a host family, so I could be a full time athlete
  • I became the first American to qualify for the Olympics and also the only female athlete that represented Karate from the US at the 2020 Toyko Olympics.
Career Highlights
  • 8x USA National Champion (Senior)
  • 4x Junior Pan American Champion
  • 4x USA National Champion (Junior)
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 5th (Individual Kata - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2023 – 5th (Individual Kata - Women)
  • Years of participation: Individual Kata - Women 2010, 2012, 2014, 2016, 2018, 2021, 2023
  • Medals: 1 (1 bronze)
    • Bronze – 2012 (Individual Kata - Women)
More
Advice to younger athletes:  Never never NEVER give up. There will be times where you just want to give up, but hard work always pays off. Just stay focused, surround yourself with good people and the result will naturally come to you.

USA Hockey

Quick Facts
  • Graduated from Wisconsin-Whitewater in 2009
  • The son of Ed and Mary Kolinske
  • One of six children. Sister, Julie, played volleyball at the University of Milwaukee
  • Left his hometown of Big Bend, Wis., to move to California and pursue beach volleyball. Prior to the move, he was a a teacher and physical education instructor at Mukwonago High School and also coached indoor volleyball at nearby Arrowhead Union
  • Also works as a tutor
Career Highlights
  • Professional debut was at the 2011 Chicago Wide Open
  • Has four podium finishes on the NORCECA Continental Tour, including one gold medal
  • ---
  • DID YOU KNOW?
  • Has played in three 2019 FIVB World Tour events with Miles Evans. Best finish was ninth at four-star in Yangzhou, China
  • Played in 15 2018 FIVB season events with Miles Evans. Placed 2nd at the one-star in Aalsmeer and third at the one-star in Sydney
  • Finished third with Evans at 2018 P1440 event in San Jose
  • Finished the 2017 FIVB World Tour with a second-place finish at the Aaismeer 1-star and a third-place at the Sydney 2-star, both with Miles Evans
  • Earned gold, silver and bronze on the 2017 NORCECA Continental Tour
  • Won bronze at the 2016 NORCECA Boqueron event with Drost, their first appearance on the Tour that year
  • Played his first FIVB World Tour event in 2015, with Casey Jennings at the Long Beach Grand Slam
  • On the NORCECA Continental Tour from 2014-16, has finished in the Top 10 in all three tournaments played
  • Has played on the AVP Tour since 2012, with three third-place finishes; was named Most Improved Player in 2015
  • First beach volleyball event of his career was at a 2010 AVP Young Guns in Milwaukee with Kyle Buckley, they finished fifth
  • Played at one tournament in 2011, the Wide Open Chicago with Drew Pickering, duo finished 17th
  • Played basketball at Wisconsin-Whitewater, graduating ranked in the top 10 in career blocks

bio.education: Wyoming Seminary University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Jim and Doreen Kolojejchick
  • Has a brother Matt, who played football at UNC
  • Best Words of Advice: I live by this quote from the movie The Replacements. Coach McGinty says, “You gotta have heart, miles and miles of heart.” You have to have it inside you to compete; you feel it in your blood and you should never just be okay with losing or be okay with having a poor performance. Have tough skin, listen and learn from criticism and mistakes, it will only make you stronger and a better athlete.
  • Favorite Sports Quote: “Winning means you’re willing to go longer, work harder, and give more than anyone else.”- Vince Lombardi
Career Highlights
  • 2016: Competed in 104 international caps for the U.S. Women's National Team
  • 2016: Series against Canada (Chula Vista, Calif.), Series against Chile (Lancaster, Pa.), Series against Holland (Hilversum, The Netherlands), Citi Rio Send-Off Series (Lancaster, Pa.)
  • 2015: Series against Argentina (Lancaster, Pa.), 8 Nations Tournament (5th, Hawke's Bay, New Zealand), Series against Ireland (Lancaster, Pa.), Series against Great Britain (Marlow, England), Series against Japan (Lancaster, Pa.)
  • 2013: Four Nations (2nd, Auckland, New Zealand)
  • 2012: Four Nations (Terrassa, Spain)
  • 2009-2012: NFHCA First Team All-American (UNC)
  • 2009: NCAA DI National Champion (UNC), ACC Freshman of the Year (UNC), Junior World Cup (Boston, Mass.)
  • 2008: Junior Pan American Games (1st, Mexico City, Mexico)
  • 2007: Named to U.S. U-21 Women's National Team
  • 2003-2005: Member of U.S. U-16 Women’s National Team
Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 5th (Team - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Gold - 2015 Pan American Games (Toronto, Canada)
  • Gold - 2014 Champions Challenge (Glasgow, Scotland)
  • Gold - 2013 World League Round 2 (Rio de Janeiro, Brazil)
  • Silver - Pan American Cup (Mendoza, Argentina)
  • Bronze - 2016 Champions Trophy (London, England)
  • 4th - 2014 Rabobank Hockey World Cup (The Hague, The Netherlands)
  • 5th - 2015 World League Round 3 (Valencia, Spain)
More
Kolojejchick first began playing field hockey in 2003 after a parent on her club soccer team suggested she that she pick up a stick. Throughout high school, she was a standout athlete not only on the field hockey pitch, but on the soccer field, the softball diamond, the gymnastics mat, the trim of track and in the swimming pool. Although with her love of difference athletics Kolojejchick ultimately chose to pursue a career in field hockey at the University of North Carolina and was the first player in the university’s history to be named as an NFHCA First Team All-American all four years. She traveled on a number of tours as a member of the U.S. Junior National Squads from 2003 to 2010 when she earned her first cap with the U.S. Women’s Senior National Squad. Since being named to the team in 2012, Kolojejchick has made an immediate impact on the pitch and has joined several of her Tarheel teammates in representing the red, white and blue.

bio.education: William Mason High School College: University of Charleston, Charleston, WV (American Football) | Northern Kentucky University Highland Heights, KY (Basketball) |  Feather River College (American Football) Quincy, CA | Ventura College (American Football) Ventura, CA
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
Matthew is certified as a Holistic Lifestyle Practitioner, Peacemaker, Strength and Conditioning and Sports Performance Coach. His mission is to unite all people he encounters with peace and love. Being a lifelong student, allows a deep reverence for the practice of implementing new lifestyle skills. Once implemented, he then uses experience from himself and others he’s coached to help them find their purpose and passion. He has worked with thousands of humans in his twenties ranging from youth to professional and olympic level athletes, as well as families and high level business people. From becoming the director of Football for Mamba Sports Academy, to consulting as an Athletic Engineer for Jumpman- Matthew utilized all of his experience to date in 2019 and co-created a holistic & athletic middle school called The Togethership in Orange County, CA. He now owns and operates three companies primarily his personal brand and holistic coaching service. His other two companies he’s co-founded are Opti Well-Being Solutions, and The Global Wellness group have common missions of optimizing the well-being of the world, one workplace at a time. Personally, Matthew takes great pride in his health, his physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual relationships with himself. These are the parts of the whole that allow Matt to perform as a high level athlete, and human being. Of equal importance to him, is his relationship with wife Kristen, his family (Father Mike, Mother Kim, Brother Patrick) and close friends and colleagues. He also owns two Dogs, Nike and Mamba and resides in Silverlake, CA. “My dream is to continue to utilize this sport as a vehicle to connect and unite the world with peace, love, and harmony. Yes, when we are between the lines, we will compete with great ferocity, and have an absolute blast while playing a little sport.”
Career Highlights
  • 2022 NACHC Continental Championships, Acapulco Mexico - 1st
  • 2022 Copa Internacional de Balonmano Playa Volcán, Colima Mexico - 2nd | Named Mejor Defensor (Defensive MVP)
  • 2021 SoCal Championships, Huntington Beach (CA) USA - 1st
  • 2021 Champions Cup, Isolla De Femme, Italy
  • 2021 Arena 1000 Handball Tour Nazare, Portugal
  • 2021 Arena 1000 Handball Tour Orihuela, Spain
  • 2020 Copa Internacional de Balonmano Playa Volcán - 1st

bio.education: Methacton High School (Eagleville, Pa.) ‘10 Georgetown University ’14, Biology
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Konkoly grew up swimming as an able-bodied athlete and competed in high school on state level relay teams. She returned to the sport 2011 after falling out of a window from a five-story dorm building at Georgetown University. She sustained an injury to her spine, a broken foot and a punctured lung. While attending Georgetown University, she won several awards including Most Resilient Female Athlete, The John Hensler Award for Most Improved Female Swimmer, Princeton University’s 'Big Al' Inspirational Swimmer Award and was named in the Big East All-Academic Team
  • Daughter of Les and Jane Konkoly
  • Has two younger sisters
  • Deferred medical school to train for the Paralympic Games
  • Has a corgi named Ollie
  • Has made and sold wedding cakes.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian; 4-time Paralympic medalist (2 gold, 1 silver, 1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, gold (100m Freestyle S9 - Women, 50m Freestyle S9 - Women), silver (4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women), bronze (4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Women)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2015 – silver (100m Freestyle S9 - Women, 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women, 50m Freestyle S9 - Women), 4th (4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Women)
  • Years of participation: 100m Freestyle S9 - Women 2015; 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women 2015; 4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Women 2015; 50m Freestyle S9 - Women 2015
  • Medals: 3 (3 silver)
    • Silver – 2015 (100m Freestyle S9 - Women, 4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women, 50m Freestyle S9 - Women)
